author: Athanasius of Alexandria

title: Prophecies of Christ's sovereignty, flight into Egypt, &c.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Prophecies of Christ’s sovereignty, flight into Egypt, &c.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But what king that ever was, before he had strength to call father or mother, reigned and gained triumphs over his enemies?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Did not David come to the throne at thirty years of age, and Solomon, when he had grown to be a young man?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Did not Joas enter on the kingdom when seven years old, and Josias, a still later king, receive the government about the seventh year of his age?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And yet they at that age had strength to call father or mother.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Who, then, is there that was reigning and spoiling his enemies almost before his birth?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or what king of this sort has ever been in Israel and in Juda—let the Jews, who have searched out the matter, tell us—in whom all the nations have placed their hopes and had peace, instead of being at enmity with them on every side?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For as long as Jerusalem stood there was war without respite betwixt them, and they all fought with Israel; the Assyrians oppressed them, the Egyptians persecuted them, the Babylonians fell upon them; and, strange to say, they had even the Syrians their neighbours at war against them.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or did not David war against them of Moab, and smite the Syrians, Josias guard against his neighbours, and Ezechias quail at the boasting of Senacherim, and Amalek make war against Moses, and the Amorites oppose him, and the inhabitants of Jericho array themselves against Jesus son of Naue?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And, in a word, treaties of friendship had no place between the nations and Israel.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Who, then, it is on whom the nations are to set their hope, it is worth while to see.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For there must be such an one, as it is impossible for the prophet to have spoken falsely.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But which of the holy prophets or of the early patriarchs has died on the Cross for the salvation of all?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or who was wounded and destroyed for the healing of all?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or which of the righteous men, or kings, went down to Egypt, so that at his coming the idols of Egypt fell?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For Abraham went thither, but idolatry prevailed universally all the same.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Moses was born there, and the deluded worship of the people was there none the less.
